title: IBPS Clerk 2026
meta_description: IBPS Clerk 2026 — clerical cadre in 11 public sector banks. Prelims Oct 10-11, Mains Dec 27, salary ₹22-35K/month, no interview, state-wise posting.

hero.one_liner: Clerical cadre recruitment for 11 public sector banks — one of the easiest banking exams with good salary and promotion to officer grade

what_is_it: IBPS Clerk is an annual recruitment exam for the post of Clerk / Junior Associate in 11 participating public sector banks. It's considered the entry-level banking exam — easier than IBPS PO and SBI PO but still offering a permanent government banking job with all benefits.

As a Clerk, you handle customer-facing operations: account opening, deposit/withdrawal, passbook updating, cheque processing, and customer queries. The work is routine and office-based — typically 10 AM to 5 PM with no field work. This makes IBPS Clerk popular among candidates who prefer a predictable, comfortable work environment.

Starting salary is ₹22,000-35,000/month (depending on city). Key benefits include: medical insurance, pension (NPS), annual increments, subsidized loans, and job security. Most importantly, Bank Clerks can appear for the JAIIB/CAIIB exams and departmental promotion exams to become Officers (Scale I) within 3-5 years without any external competitive exam.

The selection process has two stages: Prelims (online, screening) and Mains (online, final merit). There is NO interview for IBPS Clerk — selection is purely on Mains score. This makes it one of the most objective banking exams.

key_details.label: Education
key_details.value: Bachelor's degree from any recognized university (any stream)

key_details.label: Selection
key_details.value: Prelims → Mains → Provisional Allotment. NO interview.

key_details.label: Language proficiency
key_details.value: Must know the official/local language of the state you're applying for

key_details.label: Posting
key_details.value: Within the state you apply for — IBPS Clerk is state-wise allocation

exam_pattern.description: Online exam, 100 MCQs, 60 minutes. Sectional timing enforced. Qualifying round only — marks don't count toward final merit.
Online exam, 190 MCQs, 160 minutes. 4 sections with individual time limits. This determines your final merit and bank allocation.

extra_sections.heading: Clerk to Officer — Promotion Path
content: IBPS Clerk is NOT a dead-end job. The promotion path is well-defined:

Clerk (joined) → after 3-5 years → appear for JAIIB + CAIIB exams (professional qualifications from Indian Institute of Banking & Finance) → appear for internal promotion exam → promoted to Officer Scale I (same as IBPS PO/SBI PO entry level).

Once promoted to Officer, your salary jumps from ₹25K to ₹42K+ immediately. From Officer, further promotions follow the same path as a directly recruited PO: Manager → Senior Manager → Chief Manager → AGM → DGM → GM.

Many current General Managers and Executive Directors of public sector banks started their careers as Clerks. The banking industry rewards consistency and knowledge — your entry point matters less than your performance over time.
Additional paths: Some clerks appear for IBPS PO / SBI PO as external candidates while working. Banks allow study leave and exam leave for this purpose.
If selected as PO externally, you join at the PO level with a fresh start.

extra_sections.heading: IBPS Clerk vs IBPS PO — Which Should You Target?
rows: Appear for both — Clerk as backup

extra_sections.heading: State-wise Language Requirement — Critical!
content: Unlike IBPS PO where you can be posted anywhere in India, IBPS Clerk recruitment is state-specific. You apply for a specific state, and if selected, you're posted within that state only.

Critical requirement: You must know the official language or local language of the state you apply for. This is verified during document verification — you need a certificate proving your language proficiency (usually: studied the language in school, 10th/12th marksheet showing the language, or a specific language certificate).

State Languages: Hindi (multiple states), Bengali (West Bengal), Gujarati (Gujarat), Marathi (Maharashtra), Kannada (Karnataka), Malayalam (Kerala), Tamil (Tamil Nadu), Telugu (Telangana/AP), Punjabi (Punjab), Odia (Odisha), Assamese (Assam).
Tip: Apply for your home state where you naturally know the local language. If you want a different state, ensure you have proof of knowing that state's language before applying.
This is a common rejection reason that catches applicants off-guard at verification stage.
Important: Certificate should be obtained from school/college registrar or a government body. Self-written certificates are NOT accepted.

preparation_tips: IBPS Clerk Prelims is easier than PO Prelims — the cutoffs are lower and questions are less complex. If you've been preparing for PO, Clerk should feel manageable. Aim for 70+ marks in Prelims (out of 100) to be safe.

General/Financial Awareness carries 50 marks in Mains — the highest single section. Focus on: banking terminology, RBI functions, recent banking news, financial inclusion schemes, and basic economic concepts. This section has no 'preparation ceiling' — the more you know, the more you score.

Computer Aptitude is clubbed with Reasoning in Mains. Don't ignore computer knowledge — MS Office basics, internet/networking terms, and keyboard shortcuts are easy marks. Spend 1 week learning computer basics.

Local language requirement is non-negotiable. Verify you have the required language certificate BEFORE applying. Missing this document at verification means rejection despite clearing the exam. Get the certificate now — don't wait until after exam results.
Practice time management: in Mains, you have unequal time per section. Reasoning (45 min) and Aptitude (45 min) are generous. English (35 min) and GK (30 min) are tight. Practice accordingly.

faqs.question: Is IBPS Clerk a government job?
faqs.answer: Yes. IBPS Clerk is a permanent job in public sector banks which are government-owned. You get all government employee benefits: job security, pension (NPS), medical insurance, housing/HRA, LTC, and paid leaves.

faqs.question: Can I apply for multiple states?
faqs.answer: No. You can apply for only ONE state in each IBPS Clerk notification. Choose carefully — you'll be posted in that state for at least the initial years. Inter-state transfers are possible after a few years but not guaranteed.

faqs.question: Is there a typing test for IBPS Clerk?
faqs.answer: No typing test or skill test. Selection is purely based on Prelims + Mains scores. However, you should know basic computer operations for the actual job — data entry, passbook printing, and using banking software are part of daily work.

faqs.question: Clerk salary after 5 years?
faqs.answer: After 5 years with annual increments + DA revisions, a Clerk's in-hand salary reaches approximately ₹30,000-45,000/month. If promoted to Officer during this period, salary jumps to ₹50,000-65,000. Adding perks (medical, subsidized loans, LTC), the effective compensation is significantly higher.

faqs.question: Can I appear for Clerk at age 28 and PO at age 30?
faqs.answer: Yes — different age limits for different exams. Clerk age limit is 28 (Gen), PO is 30. Many candidates in the 28-30 age bracket appear for PO (still eligible) after securing Clerk as a backup. Smart strategy: clear Clerk first, then prepare for PO while working.

faqs.question: What is JAIIB/CAIIB exam?
faqs.answer: JAIIB (Junior Associate Indian Institute of Banking) and CAIIB (Certified Associate Indian Institute of Banking) are professional banking qualifications conducted by the Indian Institute of Banking & Finance. These are mandatory for clerk-to-officer promotion and test banking knowledge. Banks provide study materials and study leave for these exams.

syllabus.subject: Reasoning Ability
syllabus.topics: Seating arrangement, puzzles, syllogism, coding-decoding, blood relations, inequality, direction, order & ranking, data sufficiency, alphanumeric series

syllabus.subject: Numerical Ability
syllabus.topics: Number series, simplification, data interpretation, percentage, ratio, profit & loss, SI/CI, time & work, time & distance, probability

syllabus.subject: English Language
syllabus.topics: Reading comprehension, cloze test, error detection, sentence rearrangement, fill in blanks, phrase replacement

study_plan.tasks: IBPS Clerk is easier than IBPS PO — same syllabus but lower difficulty. Focus on speed over depth. Practice DI (15+ marks). Basic reasoning — seating and puzzles. English comprehension practice.
Sectional tests daily. Target: complete Prelims in 45 minutes (you have 60). Banking awareness — read about RBI, banking terms, current schemes. Mock tests weekly.
2-3 full mocks per week. Analyze time spent per question. Target: 75+ in Prelims for safe cutoff. Current affairs monthly compilation. Focus on accuracy.
